_status: Truth is filled in and hand-verified from the tester's photo — drop that photo in as receipt.jpg to arm the case (images are gitignored; until then it SKIPs and shows PENDING in --coverage).
_description: ΕΜΠΟΡΙΚΟ ΚΕΝΤΡΟ ΚΟΚΚΥΛΙΑ Ε.Π.Ε. (AB, Ληξούρι/Kefalonia, GR), scanned by an internal tester 2026-07-18. Greek supermarket receipt, VAT-inclusive, comma decimals. Nine product lines, three of them multi-buys ('4 X 0,34' water, '6 X 1,42' chocolate cream, '2 X 0,11' bags), plus two ΕΚΠΤΩΣΗ markdown lines (-0,31 and -0,47) interleaved BETWEEN products.
_crux: The receipt prints 'ΣΥΝΟΛΟ ΕΙΔΩΝ *18*' — eighteen UNITS, not eighteen lines. Extraction was already correct here (9 lines, 18.27 gross, 0.78 discount, printed total 17.49); the failure was ours, in the article-count guard, which compared the 9 LINES against the printed 18 and rejected the scan as count_mismatch — the user saw 'Couldn't read that receipt' five times across two days on a perfectly readable photo. Hence `outcome: ok` is the real assertion in this case: the numbers alone passed while the app rejected the receipt.
_taxNote: VAT-inclusive: the ΑΝΑΛΥΣΗ ΦΠΑ block at the foot (13% on 13.50 -> 1.76, 24% on 1.69 -> 0.40, 0% on 0.14) is a rate SUMMARY, not tax added on top, so tax = 0. It cross-checks the total: 15.33 net + 2.16 VAT = 17.49.
_discountNote: Both ΕΚΠΤΩΣΗ lines are discounts, never products: 0.31 + 0.47 = 0.78, and 18.27 gross - 0.78 = the printed 17.49.
